Datenerhebung und Fehlerquellen

Lorenz Alban Oehler

library(reactable)
library(haven)
library(VIM)

library(ggeffects)
library(performance)

processing

loading

dat_raw <- readRDS("../data/ESS_germany_selection.RDS")

dat_raw$essround |> hist(,breaks = 11)

variables

variables_models <- c("essround","idno","dweight","pspwght","anweight","prob","birth_year")
constructs <- c("stflife","happy","wrhpp")
depent_variable <- "agea"
controll_variables <- c("edulvla","edulvlb","eduyrs","eisced" ## Bildung
                        ,"hinctnta","hinctnt" ## Einkommen (log transformiert)
                        ,"marital", "maritala","maritalb" ## Famlieenstand 
                        ,"uemp3m" ## Erwerbsstatus
                        ,"health" ## subjektive Gesundheit 
                        ,"gndr" ## Geschlecht
                        )

controll_variables_selection <- c("eduyrs", "hinctnta", "maritalb", "uemp3m", "health", "gndr")

change classes

dat_raw$eduyrs |> is.numeric()
## [1] TRUE
dat_raw$maritalb <- dat_raw$maritalb |> as.factor()

list_factor_variables <- c("maritalb","uemp3m","gndr","essround")

dat_raw[,list_factor_variables] <- sapply(dat_raw[,list_factor_variables],function(x) as.factor(x))

center variable

dat_raw$agea_centered <- dat_raw$agea - mean(dat_raw$agea, na.rm = TRUE)

filter data set

dat <- dat_raw[,c(variables_models,constructs,depent_variable,controll_variables_selection)]

dat_raw |> nrow()
## [1] 36845
unique(dat_raw$idno) |> length()
## [1] 33900
(dat_raw |> nrow()) - unique(dat_raw$idno) |> length()
## [1] 2945
unique(dat_raw$idno) |> length()/((dat_raw |> nrow()))
## [1] 0.9200706

NA handeling

my_vars <- c("stflife", "agea", "eduyrs", "hinctnta", "maritalb", "uemp3m", "health", "gndr")
my_vars <- c("stflife", "agea", "eduyrs", "uemp3m", "health", "gndr")

summary_table <- dat_raw %>%
  group_by(essround) %>%
  summarise(
    total_rows = n(),
    # 'pick' only looks at the data within the current group (the current year)
    valid_cases = sum(complete.cases(pick(all_of(my_vars))))
  )

print(summary_table)
## # A tibble: 11 × 3
##    essround total_rows valid_cases
##    <chr>         <int>       <int>
##  1 1              2919        2874
##  2 10             8725        7481
##  3 11             2420        2402
##  4 2              2870        2743
##  5 3              2916        2826
##  6 4              2751        2706
##  7 5              3031        3007
##  8 6              2958        2931
##  9 7              3045        3013
## 10 8              2852        2832
## 11 9              2358        2338
dat_raw %>%
  select(essround, all_of(c("stflife", "agea", "eduyrs", "hinctnta", "maritalb", "uemp3m", "health", "gndr"))) %>%
  group_by(essround) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(!is.na(.))))
# Use your model variables
vars_to_check <- c("essround", "stflife", "agea", "eduyrs", "hinctnta", "maritalb", "uemp3m", "health","idno","gndr")

aggr(dat_raw[, vars_to_check], 
     col = c('navyblue','red'), 
     numbers = TRUE, 
     sortVars = TRUE, 
     labels = names(dat_raw[, vars_to_check]), 
     cex.axis = .7, 
     gap = 3, 
     ylab = c("Histogram of missing data","Pattern"))
## Warning in plot.aggr(res, ...): not enough vertical space to display
## frequencies (too many combinations)

## 
##  Variables sorted by number of missings: 
##  Variable       Count
##  maritalb 0.549328267
##  hinctnta 0.364038540
##    eduyrs 0.022988194
##      agea 0.018618537
##    uemp3m 0.017967160
##      gndr 0.010313475
##    health 0.003826842
##   stflife 0.003555435
##  essround 0.000000000
##      idno 0.000000000

statistical models

Models

dependent_variable <- "stflife"

# controll_variables_formula <- "eduyrs + log(hinctnta) + maritalb + uemp3m + health + gndr + gndr*agea_centered"
# controll_variables_formula <- "eduyrs + log(hinctnta) + uemp3m + health + gndr + gndr*agea + (1|idno)"
controll_variables_formula <- "eduyrs + uemp3m + health + gndr + gndr*agea_centered + (1|idno)" ### only version with 11 rounds
# 
tmp_formula_first <- as.formula(paste0(dependent_variable," ~ agea_centered + I(agea_centered^2) + ",controll_variables_formula))
tmp_formula_second <- as.formula(paste0(dependent_variable," ~ agea_centered + I(agea_centered^2) + ",controll_variables_formula, " + (1|essround)"))
tmp_formula_third <- as.formula(paste0(dependent_variable,"~ agea_centered + I(agea_centered^2) + ",controll_variables_formula, " + (1|birth_year/idno)"))
model1 <- lmer(tmp_formula_first, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght)
model2 <- lmer(tmp_formula_second, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght)
model3 <- lmer(tmp_formula_third, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght)

# model1 <- lmer(tmp_formula_first, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght,REML = FALSE)
# model2 <- lmer(tmp_formula_second, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght,REML = FALSE)
# model3 <- lmer(tmp_formula_third, data = dat_raw,weights = pspwght,REML = FALSE)

tab_model(model1, model2, model3, 
          show.aic = TRUE, 
          dv.labels = c("Model: ID only", "Model: ID + Year", "Model: BirthYear/ID"))
  Model: ID only Model: ID + Year Model: BirthYear/ID

comparison

anova(model1, model2)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
anova(model1, model3)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)
AIC(model2, model3)
BIC(model2, model3)
anova(model2,model3)
## refitting model(s) with ML (instead of REML)

Model Assumptions

Model 1

# plot_model(model2, type = "diag")[[1]]
# plot_model(model2, type = "diag")[[1]]
check_model(model1, check = "linearity")

# Nur die Homoskedastizität (Varianz) prüfen
check_model(model1, check = "homogeneity")

# Nur die Multikollinearität prüfen (Wichtig für dein Alter^2!)
check_model(model1, check = "vif")

Model 2

check_model(model2, check = "linearity")

# Nur die Homoskedastizität (Varianz) prüfen
check_model(model2, check = "homogeneity")

# Nur die Multikollinearität prüfen (Wichtig für dein Alter^2!)
check_model(model2, check = "vif")

Model 3

check_model(model3, check = "linearity")

# Nur die Homoskedastizität (Varianz) prüfen
check_model(model3, check = "homogeneity")

# Nur die Multikollinearität prüfen (Wichtig für dein Alter^2!)
check_model(model3, check = "vif")
